title: Module Check: Dynamic Programming — The Three-Stage Pipeline

questions.question: What are the two conditions required for dynamic programming to apply?
questions.answer: Optimal substructure (the optimal solution to the full problem can be built from optimal solutions to subproblems) and overlapping subproblems (the same subproblems are solved multiple times in naive recursion).

questions.question: In 0/1 Knapsack 1D DP, why must the capacity loop iterate in reverse order?
questions.answer: Forward iteration would use the already-updated dp[w - weight] value from the current item, effectively allowing the item to be chosen multiple times. Iterating backwards ensures dp[w - weight] still reflects the previous row (item not included yet), enforcing the 0/1 constraint.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of top-down memoisation over bottom-up tabulation?
questions.answer: Top-down only computes subproblems that are actually needed (lazy evaluation). For problems with sparse subproblem graphs, this can save significant time. Bottom-up always fills all states, which is wasteful if most states are unreachable.

questions.question: Which Python decorator converts a recursive function to memoised DP in one line (Python 3.9+)?
questions.explanation: `@functools.cache` is the simpler alias added in Python 3.9 — equivalent to `@lru_cache(maxsize=None)`. `@functools.lru_cache()` works in 3.8+ but requires call parentheses. `@memoize` and `@cache(maxsize=None)` are not stdlib.

questions.question: Which problem requires a 2D DP state because a 1D state is insufficient?

options.C: Buy and Sell Stock with Cooldown

questions.explanation: Stock with Cooldown needs state = (day, holding_status) — two independent dimensions. Fibonacci and Climbing Stairs only need the current index. Kadane's uses a single running max with no subproblem overlap.
